items.title: Zelenskyy: 'The most influencial Member of NATO seems to be Putin' | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has called for increased European unity in the face of the threat posed by Russian aggression and the possibility that the US under Donald Trump could withdraw its support for Kyiv.

Zelenskyy pointed to intelligence suggesting that Russia was sending troops to Belarus as opening up the possibility of an attack on other European countries besides his own.

"The time has come, the armed forces of Europe must be created," he said.

Zelenskyy also spoke about fears that the US might be planning to cut a bilateral deal with Russian President Vladimir Putin to end the conflict in Ukraine.

"Ukraine will never accept deals made behind our backs without our involvement. No decisions about Ukraine without Ukraine, no decisions about Europe without Europe," he said.

He said security guarantees for Ukraine should include either NATO membership for Ukraine or "conditions that allow us to build another NATO here in Ukraine."

He concluded his speech with a call for world peace and "peace for all your families."

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Scholz offers strong rebuke to JD Vance, reaffirms support for Ukraine | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>German Chancellor Olaf Scholz opened his speech at the Munich Security Conference with a sharp rebuke of far-right and anti-democratic forces, sending a strong message of opposition to Germany's AfD party and anyone from abroad who supports it.
It was a clear message directed at the US and Donald Trump's administration, whose main advisor, billionaire donor Elon Musk, has openly declared support for AfD.
Scholz said in Munich that Germany would not accept outside intervention in its elections. "That is not appropriate — especially not among friends and allies," he added.

The German Chancellor said that the defense of democracy against fascism and authoritarianism has been the foundation of the transatlantic relationship and called for maintaining this principle.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Central African Republic leader launches meme coin: What's behind it? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Is it cryptocurrency or just a con? The Central African Republic has just issued a meme-coin with the country's President himself praising the project on social media. Faustin-Archange Touadéra is following in the footsteps of other world leaders - specifically US President, Donald Trump, who also issued a meme coin, as did his wife, Melania.
Back in 2022 CAR became only the second country worldwide to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender, alongside the CFA Franc. But this new meme coin's own website sets out some interesting details under frequently asked questions. At the end is small print saying the meme coin is "not intended to function as an investment, security, or financial instrument of any kind, has no intrinsic value and should not be viewed as a financial asset."

00:00 CAR launches meme coin
03:06 DW speaks with Tony Olendo, acting chair of Virtual Assets Chamber of Commerce, a policy think tank advising African governments on virtual assets, which including crypto currencies.


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: What is India's perspective on current global security issues? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>India is backing Donald Trump’s plans to launch peace talks with Vladimir Putin. Speaking to DW’s Richard Walker at the Munich Security Conference, BJP foreign affairs chief Vijay Chauthaiwale said New Delhi would “do whatever is required for peace” – rejecting a “blame game” over responsibilty for the war and not ruling out India contributing troops to an international force in Ukraine after any cease-fire. While the US Vice President attacked European democracies in his Munich speech, exposing a gulf in transatlantic relations, Chauthaiwale stressed the similarities between Donald Trump’s MAGA movement and Narendra Modi’s BJP in India, where Modi long ago coined the phrase “India First.” But if every nation is out for itself, are clashes in interests inevitable? Chauthaiwale concedes Trump’s decision to leave the Paris Climate Change Agreement was a “hurdle.”

#MSC2025 #BJP #India
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: 'The Americans openly interfere in an election': Interview with chancellor candidate Friedrich Merz
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Following a provocative speech by US Vice President JD Vance at the Munich Security Conference, CDU Chairman and Union chancellor candidate Friedrich Merz spoke with DW correspondent Michaela Küfner. Merz firmly rejected Vance's claim that democratic values and freedom of opinion are being restricted in Europe. At the same time, he reiterated that European states must do more for their own security.

items.title: Women leaders discuss European and global security at MSC | Berlin Briefing Podcast
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>How do women leaders in Europe view current security challenges? Will the US be a reliable ally to Europe? Are democracies around the world under threat?   

DW's Berlin Briefing and One Decision Podcast teamed up to host a powerful panel at the Munich Security Conference: Latvian Foreign Minister Baiba Braze, former Icelandic Foreign Minister Thordis Gylfadottir, and former Deputy Prime Minister of the Netherlands Kajsa Ollongren share their perspectives with hosts Nina Haase and Christina Ruffini.

items.title: "The US will do less and we have to do more" German Defense Minister Pistorius | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius has sharply criticized a speech by US Vice President JD Vance at the Munich Security Conference. JD Vance said that free speech was "in retreat" in Europe. Pistorius said, ''It's about his opinion about democracies. And I said, no, this is not acceptable to accuse us [of] not being real Democrats. And I had to make that clear as a representative of a German government here in Munich.''

items.title: German Defense Minister Pistorius rebuffs US Vice President Vance's speech | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius has heavily criticized JD Vance after the US vice president accused European governments of ignoring voter concerns on immigration and stamping down on free speech.

"Democracy was called into question by the US vice president for the whole of Europe earlier," Pistorius said at the Munich Security Conference. "He speaks of the annihilation of democracy. And if I have understood him correctly, he is comparing conditions in parts of Europe with those in authoritarian regions... that is not acceptable." Pistorius accused Vance of painting a distorted picture of democracy. With the German elections just over a week away, and the far-right AfD in second place in the polls, Pistorius poured scorn on the notion that free speech was being stymied. Democracy means that "extremist parties such as the AfD can campaign quite normally, just like any other party. However, democracy does not mean that the loud minority is automatically right and determines the truth," Pistorius said. Earlier, JD Vance said that free speech was "in retreat" in Europe while mass migration was a topic being underestimated by nations across the continent.

Chapters:
00:00 German Defense Minister Pistorius rebuffs US VP Vance
02:30 Teri Schulz, DW Correspondent
06:25 Cathryn Clüver Ashbrook, Bertelsmann Foundation



#msc2025 #Pistorius #JDVance

items.title: Munich Security Conference 2025 Day 1, with JD Vance, Ursula von der Leyen, Wang Yi | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>From February 14-16, 2025, the 61st Munich Security Conference is the center of international diplomacy. The MSC will offer a platform for high-level debates on the key foreign and security policy challenges of our time.

US Vice President JD Vance attends as part of his first foreign trip in post. He will be accompanied by US Foreign Secretary Marco Rubio. Vance is scheduled to meet Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on the sidelines of the conference that is expected to mainly focus on peace talks between Kyiv and Moscow.

President Donald Trump told reporters on Thursday, US officials will also meet Russian counterparts at the Munich conference. There were no further details about said meetings.

German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier opens the conference. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, EU High Representative Kaja Kallas; NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte; US Special Envoy for Russia and Ukraine Keith Kellogg; and German Chancellor Olaf Scholz and all G7 foreign ministers are expected to attend.

Chapters
00:00 Welcoming with remarks by President Steinmeier and State Premier Soder.
46:15 EU in The World with EC President Ursula von der Leyen. 
01:10:00 The US in the World with US Vice-President JD Vance. 
01:41:30 China in the World, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi takes part. 
02:16:00 Towards a Stronger UN in a Fragmented World
03:04:00 Seismic Shifts: More European Responsibility for Defense, with Federal Minister of Defence Boris Pistorius
04:07:45 Live to Vote Another Day - Fortifying Democratic Resilience
05:03:45 From Ashes to Action: Climate Cooperation in a Heated World

President Donald Trump told reporters on Thursday, US officials will also meet Russian counterparts at the Munich conference. There were no further details about said meetings.

German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier opens the conference. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, EU High Representative Kaja Kallas; NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte; US Special Envoy for Russia and Ukraine Keith Kellogg; and German Chancellor Olaf Scholz and all G7 foreign ministers are expected to attend.
